steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that omitting the subject's elitism and ideological complexity "establishes him as a compelling and well-defined protagonist." The correct answer must name the effect of that omission.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To suggest why certain contributions by Hamilton to US history have been overlooked.: describes an effect the passage does not identify. ✗
  • Choice B — To encourage modern audiences to emulate Hamilton's politics.: describes an effect the passage does not identify. ✗
  • Choice C — To create an unambiguous thematic presentation of Hamilton's life.: a well-defined protagonist free of internal contradiction is precisely an unambiguous presentation — matches the passage directly. ✓
  • Choice D — To challenge the prevailing historical consensus about Hamilton.: describes an effect the passage does not identify. ✗
That is the correct answer.
